mechanism_narrative: Persistent elevation in consumer price index levels, as measured by CPIAUCSL, signals ongoing inflation pressure that systematically pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days as investors demand higher compensation for inflation risk.
failure_condition: Rank IC over 3-year rolling window remains below 0.03 or proportion of correct direction calls in out-of-sample triggered events is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below the 5.5% threshold indicates labor market tightening that fosters wage inflation, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise within 5 trading days as rate hike expectations intensify.
failure_condition: In at least 60% of triggered instances the 10y yield moves down or remains flat, or the average yield change is negative over 5-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Joint observation of near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and elevated CPI (CPIAUCSL) produces negative real interest rates, which drives investors to sell Treasuries, pushing yields higher over 20 trading days as expected inflation accelerates.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal (product of standardised values) below 0.05 over any 3-year window or the yield response is negative in more than 50% of observations when both conditions hold.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, rising real GDP (GDPC1) reflects aggregate demand growth that, without offsetting supply, pushes up long-term yields over a 60-trading-day horizon as growth expectations become embedded in term premiums.
failure_condition: Yield increases in fewer than 55% of 60-day windows during identified recovery regimes, or the correlation between GDP growth surprises and yield changes is not positive and significant (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals increasing capacity utilization and economic momentum, which raises the natural rate of interest and pushes the 10-year yield upward over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for INDPRO direction signal < 0.04 over any 2-year window or the mean yield change when INDPRO rises is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A surge in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 7.0% signals the onset of a recessionary contraction, leading to flight-to-quality demand for Treasuries that pushes yields lower over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In at least 50% of triggered instances the 10y yield rises instead of falls, or the average yield change over 20 days is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When both payroll employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) are expanding concurrently, it signals robust consumer-driven demand that raises long-term yield expectations through aggregate demand pressure over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint signal below 0.03 over 3-year window or the yield change is negative in more than 45% of observations when both series show positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound regime where the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains at its effective lower bound for an extended period, market expectations of prolonged accommodative policy anchor the 10-year yield at low levels, with a tendency to drift further down over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 10-year yield increases in more than 40% of 20-day windows during the regime, or the average yield change is positive, contradicting the predicted downward drift.
